test_1_introspection.payload: {"query":"{ __schema { types { name fields { name type { name kind ofType { name kind } } } } } }"}

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: GraphQL introspection has been disabled, but the requested query contained the field "__schema".

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: GraphQL introspection has been disabled, but the requested query contained the field "types".

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: GraphQL introspection has been disabled, but the requested query contained the field "fields".

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: GraphQL introspection has been disabled, but the requested query contained the field "type".

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: GraphQL introspection has been disabled, but the requested query contained the field "kind".

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: GraphQL introspection has been disabled, but the requested query contained the field "ofType".

test_1_introspection.finding: Introspection is properly disabled. However, error messages individually confirm each field name exists in the schema (leak).

test_2_query_without_auth.description: Attempt to query users without authentication

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: Cannot query field "users" on type "Query". [Suggestion hidden]?

test_2_query_without_auth.finding: Field 'users' does not exist on Query. However, 'user' (singular) DOES exist and requires auth.

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: GraphQL introspection has been disabled, but the requested query contained the field "__type".

errors.name: ValidationError
errors.message: Cannot query field "login" on type "Mutation". [Suggestion hidden]?

test_3b_login_mutation.finding: No 'login' mutation exists. Mutation type confirmed to exist.

test_4_batch_queries.description: Batch query attack - multiple queries in single request
test_4_batch_queries.finding: CRITICAL: Batch queries are supported. Server processed both queries independently. This enables potential DoS via query amplification and bypasses per-request rate limiting.
test_4_batch_queries.security_impact: Batch queries can be used to amplify attack surface, bypass rate limiting, and potentially cause DoS through query multiplication.

test_5_sql_injection.description: SQL injection attempt via GraphQL arguments
test_5_sql_injection.finding: No SQL injection via 'name' argument (argument doesn't exist). However, this confirmed: (1) 'user' field exists on Query, (2) 'User' type exists, (3) 'id' is NOT a field on User type (or requires auth).
test_5_sql_injection.info_leak: Error messages confirm schema structure without introspection.

test_6_rate_limiting.description: 20 rapid requests to check rate limiting
test_6_rate_limiting.finding: CRITICAL: No rate limiting detected. All 20 requests returned HTTP 200 with zero throttling. Enables brute force, enumeration, and DoS attacks.
test_6_rate_limiting.security_impact: No rate limiting allows: credential stuffing, token brute force, query flooding, enumeration attacks.

schema_enumeration_via_errors.description: Fields and types discovered through validation error messages (introspection bypass)

user.known_args: none discovered (id, name are NOT valid args)

security_findings_summary.title: GraphQL Batch Queries Enabled - Rate Limit Bypass & DoS
security_findings_summary.description: The GraphQL endpoint accepts batch queries (array of queries in single request). All 20 rapid single requests returned 200 with no rate limiting. Batch queries amplify this by allowing multiple operations per request.
security_findings_summary.evidence: Batch: [{query1},{query2}] processed both. Rate limit test: 20/20 returned 200.
security_findings_summary.impact: Rate limit bypass, query amplification DoS, enumeration acceleration.
security_findings_summary.recommendation: Disable batch queries. Implement rate limiting per IP/session. Add query depth/complexity limits.

security_findings_summary.title: No Rate Limiting on GraphQL Endpoint
security_findings_summary.description: The GraphQL endpoint at /graphql applies zero rate limiting. 20 rapid-fire requests all returned HTTP 200.
security_findings_summary.evidence: 20 consecutive POST requests to /graphql all returned 200 in rapid succession.
security_findings_summary.impact: Enables credential stuffing, brute force attacks, API abuse, and denial of service.
security_findings_summary.recommendation: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 100 req/min per IP). Add exponential backoff. Consider WAF rules.

security_findings_summary.title: Verbose GraphQL Error Messages Leak Schema Information
security_findings_summary.description: Despite introspection being disabled, validation error messages reveal field names, type names, argument types, and service architecture. This effectively provides a partial schema map.
security_findings_summary.evidence: Error responses leak: field names (user, accounts, investment, settings), type names (User, Settings, Mutation, Subscription), argument types (id: ID!), service name (graphql_service), infrastructure (envoy).
security_findings_summary.impact: Attackers can enumerate the full schema through systematic error triggering, bypassing introspection protection.
security_findings_summary.recommendation: Use generic error messages. Suppress field/type names in production errors. Return only 'Cannot query field X on type Y' without suggesting alternatives.

security_findings_summary.title: Infrastructure Information Disclosure
security_findings_summary.description: HTTP headers and TLS certificate reveal infrastructure details.
security_findings_summary.evidence: Server: envoy, cert CN: masterssl.acorns.com, AWS IPs (us-east-1), TLS 1.3, HTTP/2.
security_findings_summary.impact: Aids reconnaissance for targeted attacks against known envoy/AWS vulnerabilities.
security_findings_summary.recommendation: Remove server header. Use generic certificate CN. Consider CloudFlare/WAF in front.

security_findings_summary.title: Persisted Query Support Enabled
security_findings_summary.description: The server accepts persisted query extensions, returning PERSISTED_QUERY_NOT_FOUND for unknown hashes.
security_findings_summary.evidence: Request with extensions.persistedQuery returned specific error code PERSISTED_QUERY_NOT_FOUND.
security_findings_summary.impact: If predictable hash algorithms are used, attackers may guess valid persisted queries. Also confirms Apollo-style server.
security_findings_summary.recommendation: Ensure persisted query hashes are not guessable. Consider disabling if not needed.

fake_bearer_token.response: Same auth error - no difference in behavior
fake_bearer_token.finding: Server does not distinguish between missing and invalid tokens at the GraphQL layer.

ip_spoofing.finding: IP spoofing headers do not bypass authentication.
